differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinuria) due to chronic acetaminophen and malnutrition
Acute-on-chronic heart failure leading to hypoperfusion
Hypercapnic encephalopathy due to respiratory decompensation
next_steps: Measure serum anion gap, serum ketones (beta-hydroxybutyrate), and arterial blood gas
Check serum acetaminophen and lactate levels
Evaluate for 5-oxoproline in the urine (organic acid analysis)
Aggressive resuscitation of shock and optimization of ventilator support
Inpatient neurology consultation for altered mental status evaluation

differential_diagnoses: Sepsis (urosepsis) leading to lactic acidosis (Type A)
Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinuria) related to chronic acetaminophen use and malnutrition
Adrenal insufficiency secondary to chronic corticosteroid use
Hypercapnic encephalopathy due to respiratory decompensation
Acute-on-chronic heart failure with cardiogenic shock
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas, serum electrolytes (including anion gap), and beta-hydroxybutyrate
Check serum cortisol level for adrenal insufficiency evaluation
Perform urinary organic acid analysis (specifically checking for 5-oxoproline)
Hold all potentially contributing medications (dapagliflozin, metformin, acetaminophen)
Ensure source control for potential urosepsis (urology consultation for bladder drainage)
Continue aggressive fluid resuscitation and vasopressor support for septic/cardiogenic shock
Obtain urine culture and toxicology screen for metabolic and toxicological contributors
